scenario: An Aurora Bank operations team reviews a stuck process instance in Camunda 8.8 Operate where 12 running tokens are waiting on a single user task after a downstream payments-gateway outage of 4 hours. The team wants to cancel every running token on that one flow node at once rather than picking each token individually from the instance history panel.
question: How does an operator cancel all running tokens on a single flow node from Operate's modification mode?

options.text: Select the flow node in the diagram and click Cancel instance from the dropdown so all running tokens on that node are scheduled for cancellation as a pending modification
Open each token row in the instance history panel and use the Cancel instance dropdown on every selected row before applying the modifications to the running instance
Select the flow node in the diagram and choose Add from the dropdown so all running tokens on that node are scheduled for cancellation as a pending modification
Switch to the variables panel and remove the local scope of the flow node so the engine cancels all running tokens nested under that node on the next refresh

a.text: Correct. Operate's modification mode lets an operator select a flow node in the diagram and click Cancel instance from the dropdown, which schedules cancellation of every running token on that node as a single pending modification visible in the instance history.

b.text: Incorrect. The dropdown action on the diagram flow node already cancels all running tokens on that node at once, so iterating row-by-row through the instance history panel is not the workflow that produces this bulk cancellation.

c.text: Incorrect. Add from the flow node dropdown creates a new token on the selected node; it does not cancel any running tokens. The matching action for cancelling running tokens on a node is Cancel instance from the dropdown.

d.text: Incorrect. Deleting variables from a scope does not cancel any token. Cancellation of all running tokens on a flow node is queued only by an explicit Cancel instance modification chosen from that flow node's dropdown.

explanation: To cancel all running tokens on a flow node in Operate, the operator selects the flow node in the diagram and clicks Cancel instance from the dropdown. The pending modification is then reflected in the instance history before being applied.
